title: Doctor Module Registry
description: The canonical, static list of Doctor Module Registry descriptors — Phase 3/WHIMS Batch WPI-3 (docs/50-PHASE-3-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §7.1/§19, ADR-020, docs/53-PHASE-3-IMPLEMENTATION-RULES.md). Phase 3/WHIMS's Pillar 2 (docs/49 §4) — the platform's only mechanism for expressing which doctor-facing capabilities exist at all (availability), structurally parallel to shared/constants/module-registry.json's own 'availability, not enablement' framing, applied to a separate registry for a separate identity type (Doctor Identity, ADR-017) — never merged with the patient-facing registry (ADR-020). Per-doctor enablement is a separate, Sheet-backed concern (shared/schemas/doctor-module-state.schema.json). Deliberately seeded empty in this batch: no concrete doctor-facing capability (patient roster, condition assignment, care-plan authoring, module/calculator/template enablement, inventory, PillFill orders, analytics, or any other) is registered here — this batch's own scope is the generic registry-and-state mechanism only, mirroring shared/constants/calculator-registry.json's own disclosed 'ships empty' precedent exactly. A future capability is added as its own registry entry by whichever later, separately-approved WPI batch actually builds a real doctor-facing data_source route for it (docs/53 §4's 'a new registry entry, never new architecture'), the same discipline module-registry.json's and calculator-registry.json's own header comments already established for their own not-yet-built capabilities. apps-script/DoctorModuleRegistry.gs hand-ports this list into its own Foundation-prefixed array, the same convention module-registry.json's and calculator-registry.json's own consumers already use. See doctor-module-registry.md for the full field-by-field explanation (once a future batch populates an entry) and the disclosed 'ships empty' scope decision.
